name: NavCardMenuItem
description: GNAV navigation column (aka Nav List Container). Title 4 heading + list of text links + the shared NavCardButton CTA. Mirrors the Navigation — A.com artboard (node 3872:5474) and snaps everything back to the S2A spacing/typography tokens.

composedOf.note: Each nav card menu item row is a matt-atoms Link/Text instance (node 3624:58143) with Default → Hover underline animation. Once the Link atom exists in code, reuse it here so underline, focus ring, and typography tokens stay centralized.
NavCardButton is the shared pill CTA used across all GNAV cards/menu items.

props.name: title
props.description: Title 4 heading that names the menu column (e.g. "Creative Cloud").

props.name: items
props.type: Array<{ label: string; href: string; state?: string; showIconEnd?: boolean }>
props.description: Ordered nav entries rendered as Link/Text atoms. Limit to 6 items to keep within the 288px column height from Figma.

props.name: ctaLabel
props.description: Label for the pill CTA button placed below the links.

props.name: ctaHref
props.description: Destination for the CTA button. When omitted the button renders as <button> for local behavior.

tokenFlags.note: Navigation file still references its local "2 XS" token. We map it to the S2A primitive to stay on-grid.
Both padding + heading spacing still point to the Navigation (s2c) spacing collection. Implementation should use the S2A spacing tokens.
Figma instance references s2c spacing for nav link padding. We snap to --s2a-spacing-xs (8px) so the card keeps the same rhythm as other GNAV surfaces.

tokenFlags.figmaValue: Figma shows 7px row spacing and icon gap
tokenFlags.note: Implementation standardizes both the row stack gap and label⟷chevron gap to the 8px semantic spacing token to stay on the 4px grid.

layerNames..root: Rounded white card wrapper (342×353) with 24px padding in the redesign shell
layerNames..heading: Auto-layout column with the Title 4 text node
layerNames..title: Title 4 text layer bound to Adobe Clean Display Black 24/24
layerNames..list: Stack of nav card link rows (Link/Text atoms)
layerNames..item: Individual Link instance with underline hover
layerNames..cta-stack: Bottom padding block that nests the Nav Button
layerNames..cta: Outlined pill button reused from NavCard

dimensions.width: NavCardShell locks to 340px with a 240px min (no max)
dimensions.height: 353px per Figma sample (list grows with additional items)
dimensions.note: Shell abstraction keeps CTA spacing identical to the promo card. Width + padding are centralized in NavCardShell; this spec focuses on the heading/list stack tokens.

keyboard: Tab cycles through each Link item in order, bottom CTA button last
Enter/Space activates whichever link/button currently holds focus
Arrow navigation is not provided — list relies on standard tab order
notes: Ensure each nav item label is meaningful on its own or add visually hidden context (e.g. "Creative Cloud – Photography").
If the card title should be announced for the CTA, expose it via aria-labelledby on the list container and button.
Maintain 4.5:1 contrast for nav item text (Adobe Clean 14/18) against background-default.
